user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: clean scientific visualization, realistic aircraft wing with semi-transparent airflow streamlines, minimal color palette with blue airflow and neutral wing
style_bible.camera_rules: smooth cinematic motion, no abrupt cuts, prioritize readability during holds, consistent left-to-right airflow screen direction
style_bible.lighting_rules: soft daylight key from upper-right, gentle fill to avoid harsh contrast, no dramatic shadows
style_bible.continuity_rules: same wing section, same altitude above clouds, same airflow direction, clip 2 begins exactly on clip 1 final frame without jump

clips.prompt:
CLIP INTENT: Introduce Bernoulli’s principle using a single airplane wing and airflow visualization.
GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Primary subject is a mid-wing airplane wing section viewed from the side; environment is high-altitude sky with soft clouds; camera maintains left-to-right airflow direction, medium distance; explanatory state begins observational and calm.
SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium side profile of wing occupying right half of frame
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly
- subject & action: By 1s the wing is fully in view; by 2s blue airflow lines approach smoothly; by 3s flow begins to split over and under the wing
- on-screen text overlay: none
- continuity note: airflow direction and wing position continue into next shot
- environment details: thin cloud layer below, pale blue sky
- lighting: soft daylight from upper-right
- cinematic look: crisp focus, moderate depth of field
SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: same composition, slightly closer
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion)
- subject & action: Wing remains perfectly stable; airflow lines above wing subtly accelerate while lower lines stay evenly spaced, motion calm and optimized for reading
- on-screen text overlay: "Faster air above wing"
- continuity note: airflow speed difference persists into next shot
- environment details: stable cloud texture, faint atmospheric haze
- lighting: unchanged soft daylight
- cinematic look: clean educational clarity
SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: slight pullback to include more airflow
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly
- subject & action: Airflow separation is clearer; upper streamlines stretch longer, lower remain denser, reinforcing contrast
- on-screen text overlay: none
- continuity note: final airflow configuration becomes the starting frame of clip 2
- environment details: continuous sky gradient, distant clouds
- lighting: consistent soft key
- cinematic look: smooth motion, realistic rendering
FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing side profile with clearly faster airflow above and slower airflow below, frozen at mid-flight, left-to-right flow
clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.prompt:
CLIP INTENT: Explain pressure difference and resulting lift, continuing directly from previous frame.
GLOBAL CONTINUITY: Primary subject remains the same wing and airflow from clip 1 final frame; environment unchanged high-altitude sky; camera keeps same side view, distance, and left-to-right direction; explanatory state deepens understanding.
SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: identical to clip 1 final frame
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: static
- subject & action: Airflow continues seamlessly; by 2s subtle pressure shading appears above and below wing
- on-screen text overlay: none
- continuity note: pressure shading sets up explanation hold
- environment details: same clouds and sky gradient
- lighting: soft daylight maintained
- cinematic look: seamless continuation
SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: unchanged for clarity
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: static explanatory hold (no camera motion)
- subject & action: Wing remains motionless; upper region shows lighter color indicating lower pressure, lower region darker for higher pressure, motion calm and optimized for reading
- on-screen text overlay: "Lower pressure above"
- continuity note: pressure contrast leads into lift visualization
- environment details: stable cloud layer, clear sky
- lighting: even, glare-free
- cinematic look: textbook-clean visualization
SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: slight zoom out to include lift arrow
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly
- subject & action: A clean upward arrow appears beneath wing showing lift; airflow remains steady, reinforcing cause-and-effect
- on-screen text overlay: "Pressure difference creates lift"
- continuity note: ends explanation with stable lifted wing
- environment details: clouds drifting slowly below
- lighting: consistent daylight
- cinematic look: calm, authoritative finish
FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing in steady flight with visible upward lift arrow and stable airflow, held cleanly for end
clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text
clips.camera: locked side view, seamless continuation from clip 1

full_script: 0–3s: Establish airplane wing in flight with visible airflow. 3–7s: Hold to introduce idea that faster air above wing matters. Overlay 1 appears. 7–10s: Resume motion showing airflow splitting. 10–13s: Continue same frame, reveal faster airflow above wing. 13–17s: Hold to explain pressure difference. Overlay 2 appears. 17–20s: Show resulting lift arrow and stable wing, preparing a clear ending frame.
